key_provisions: Citizens can request info from public authorities within 30 days
text: Right to Information Act 2005 (Act 22 of 2005). Ministry of Personnel. Every citizen can request information from public authorities. 30-day response deadline. ₹10 application fee. Central and State Information Commissions. Repealed Official Secrets Act misuse. Landmark transparency law.

key_provisions: Unified indirect tax replacing 17 taxes, GST Council
text: GST Acts 2017 (CGST, SGST, IGST). Ministry of Finance. Unified indirect tax system. Replaced VAT, service tax, excise, and 14 other levies. Four slabs: 5%, 12%, 18%, 28%. GST Council is constitutional body. E-way bill for goods >₹50,000. Implemented July 1, 2017.

Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery) Act 2016 (Act 18 of 2016). 12-digit unique identity number. Biometric and demographic authentication. Enables Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT). 140 crore Aadhaar numbers issued. Supreme Court upheld validity in Puttaswamy case (2018) with conditions.

key_provisions: 100 days guaranteed wage employment per rural household per year
text: Mahatma Gandhi NREGA 2005 (Act 42 of 2005). Ministry of Rural Development. Guarantees 100 days of wage employment per rural household per year. Demand-driven scheme. ₹73,000 crore budget FY24. 15 crore active job cards. Largest employment guarantee program globally.

key_provisions: Free and compulsory education for children 6-14 years
text: Right to Education Act 2009 (Act 35 of 2009). Free and compulsory education for children aged 6-14 years. 25% seats in private schools reserved for EWS under Section 12(1)(c). No detention policy up to Class 8. Pupil-teacher ratio norms. Constitutional backing from Article 21A.

key_provisions: FSSAI established, food quality standards, licensing of food businesses
text: Food Safety and Standards Act 2006. Established FSSAI as regulatory authority. All food businesses require FSSAI license. Standards for food quality, hygiene, and labeling. Penalties for adulteration up to ₹10 lakh and imprisonment.

key_provisions: CSR mandate, women director, independent directors, NCLT
text: Companies Act 2013 (Act 18 of 2013). Mandatory CSR for companies with >₹500 crore turnover (2% of net profit). At least one woman director on board. Independent directors requirement. NCLT established. One Person Company concept introduced.

key_provisions: E-commerce regulation, product liability, Central Consumer Protection Authority
text: Consumer Protection Act 2019 (Act 35 of 2019). Replaced 1986 Act. E-commerce consumer protection rules. Product liability provisions. Established Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA). Celebrity endorser liability. Mediation as dispute resolution mechanism.

key_provisions: Replaced IPC, CrPC, and Evidence Act. Zero FIR, e-reporting, video trials
text: New Criminal Laws 2023: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) replaced IPC, Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) replaced CrPC, Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam (BSA) replaced Evidence Act. Effective July 1, 2024. Key: Zero FIR, e-FIR, mandatory videography of crime scenes, community service as punishment.

key_provisions: Criminalizes bribery, corruption by public servants, Lokpal
text: Prevention of Corruption Act 1988 (Act 49 of 1988, amended 2018). Criminalizes bribery and corruption by public servants. Both bribe-giver and taker are liable (2018 amendment). Prior sanction needed to prosecute. CBI is primary investigating agency. Lokpal Act 2013 supplements this.

key_provisions: Time-bound insolvency resolution, NCLT as adjudicating authority, IBBI
text: Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code 2016 (Act 31 of 2016). Time-bound resolution: 180 days (+90 extension). NCLT as adjudicating authority. Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) as regulator. Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP). Resolved ₹3.16 lakh crore by FY24.

key_provisions: Data Protection Board, consent-based processing, ₹250 cr penalty
text: Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 (Act 22 of 2023). India's first comprehensive data protection law. Consent-based data processing. Data Principal rights: access, correction, erasure. Data Protection Board of India. ₹250 crore maximum penalty. Cross-border data transfer provisions.
